touches.ai_summary: Cold outreach to Javier Le introducing our automation platform for CS team productivity. No response received.
LinkedIn follow-up highlighting use case for CS workflow automation. No response received.
Third touchpoint via email sharing case study on CS team efficiency gains. Javier responded expressing interest in learning more about automation capabilities.

questions_asked.question: What types of workflows can your platform automate for customer success teams?
questions_asked.answer: Our unlimited automation tier supports ticket routing, escalation workflows, customer health scoring, renewal reminders, and custom integrations with your existing stack.

next_steps: Schedule intro call to discuss SpiralConsulting's current CS workflows

touches.ai_summary: Follow-up email confirming discovery call and sharing agenda. Javier confirmed availability and mentioned current pain points with manual CS processes.

questions_asked.question: How long does typical implementation take for a team our size?
questions_asked.answer: For 6 seats with standard integrations, implementation typically takes 2-3 weeks including training and workflow setup.

next_steps: Discovery call scheduled for next week

touches.ai_summary: Discovery call focused on SpiralConsulting's CS team structure and current manual workflows. Javier outlined repetitive tasks consuming 15+ hours per week. Discussed automation potential for ticket triage and customer outreach sequences.

questions_asked.question: What tools does your CS team currently use daily?
questions_asked.answer: We use Zendesk for ticketing, HubSpot CRM, and Slack. Everything else is pretty manual with spreadsheets.

questions_asked.question: Can we integrate with our existing Zendesk and HubSpot setup?
questions_asked.answer: Yes, we have native integrations with both platforms that sync bidirectionally in real-time.

next_steps: Schedule technical evaluation to review automation workflows

touches.ai_summary: Technical evaluation session (rescheduled once) demonstrating automation builder and integration setup. Javier built sample workflows for ticket routing and customer health alerts. Expressed enthusiasm about time savings but mentioned needing to discuss with leadership before moving forward.

questions_asked.question: How customizable are the automation templates?
questions_asked.answer: Fully customizable with our visual workflow builder. You can modify any template or build from scratch with conditional logic, delays, and multi-step sequences.

questions_asked.question: What's included in your support package?
questions_asked.answer: Unlimited automation tier includes priority support, dedicated onboarding specialist, and access to our workflow template library.

questions_asked.question: Who else should be involved in evaluating this for your team?
questions_asked.answer: I'll need to loop in our COO eventually, but I want to understand pricing first before bringing this upstairs.

objections: Want to see ROI projections before involving executive leadership
next_steps: Send ROI calculator based on time savings discussed

touches.ai_summary: Proposal review call (rescheduled once) covering $5,900 ACV for 6 seats with unlimited automation. Javier focused heavily on pricing breakdown and contract terms. Asked about discount options and expressed concern about budget approval without executive buy-in.

questions_asked.question: Is there flexibility on the annual commitment pricing?
questions_asked.answer: For annual contracts we can offer a 10% discount compared to monthly billing. This brings the effective monthly cost down from $590 to $492.

questions_asked.question: What happens if we need to add more seats mid-contract?
questions_asked.answer: You can add seats anytime at prorated cost. Additional seats are billed at the same per-seat rate as your original contract.

questions_asked.question: What's your timeline for getting this implemented?
questions_asked.answer: Ideally we'd want to start within the next 4-6 weeks if budget gets approved. I'm planning to present this internally in the next week or two.

objections: Budget authority limited without executive sponsor approval
Concerned about justifying cost without formal business case
next_steps: Javier to present internally to COO
Follow up in one week on executive feedback

touches.ai_summary: Negotiation call (rescheduled once) to discuss contract finalization. Javier mentioned he hasn't been able to get meeting time with COO yet but is still interested. Requested additional discount to 'make the numbers work' and extended payment terms. Discussion remained focused on pricing rather than implementation or business value.

questions_asked.question: Can you offer any additional discount beyond the 10% annual commitment?
questions_asked.answer: I can check with leadership about a first-year discount for early commitment. What budget range would make this an easier approval for you?

questions_asked.question: Would you be open to quarterly payment terms instead of annual upfront?
questions_asked.answer: We typically require annual payment, but for qualified customers we can discuss quarterly installments. This would reduce the upfront cash commitment.

questions_asked.question: Have you had a chance to connect with your COO about moving forward?
questions_asked.answer: Not yet, their schedule has been packed. I'm trying to get all the details sorted first so I can present a complete picture when we do meet.

questions_asked.question: Is there anyone else on the team who should be involved in this conversation?
questions_asked.answer: Right now it's just me evaluating this. Once I get internal alignment on budget, I'll bring in our ops lead for the technical implementation side.

objections: Price still feels high without demonstrated executive support
Difficulty scheduling time with decision-maker creating delays
next_steps: Rep to propose revised pricing with extended payment terms
Javier to secure meeting with COO within two weeks
